session_summary: Continuation of Phase 1 work on Verascope, an offline Tauri v2 + React 19 + Rust desktop app that reads/validates C2PA provenance manifests into a three-state verdict (Verified / UntrustedOrBroken / NoProvenance). This session did three discrete things: (1) authored CLAUDE.md via /init by reading docs/PROJECT.md, docs/last-session.md, and the actual source; (2) picked up the mid-wiring state left by the prior session — the Rust backend already exposed analyze_media but src/App.tsx was still the default scaffold calling the removed greet command — and wired the full frontend (new src/types.ts, rewritten App.tsx with dialog file-picker + native Tauri drag-drop, three-state verdict card, collapsible raw-manifest viewer, and a visually-separated disabled Phase 2 heuristic stub; rewritten App.css; index.html title). Frontend passed bunx tsc --noEmit and bun run build; (3) diagnosed and fixed a Rust compile error (E0255 duplicate __cmd__analyze_media) the user hit on bun run tauri dev. Root cause was the command being declared pub fn — tauri-macros re-exports the generated helper macro for pub commands, colliding with generate_handler! in the same module. Fix: pub fn -> fn. cargo check then returned exit 0 (one non-fatal c2pa deprecation warning remaining).
key_statistics: Files created: 3 (CLAUDE.md, src/types.ts, docs/handoff.json). Files rewritten: 2 (src/App.tsx, src/App.css). Files edited: 2 (index.html title, src-tauri/src/lib.rs one-word fix). Compile errors resolved: 1 (E0255, 2 error instances). Warnings remaining: 1 (Reader::from_stream deprecation). Verifications run: bunx tsc --noEmit (exit 0), bun run build (exit 0, 37 modules, ~1.96s), cargo check --no-default-features (exit 0 after fix, ~1.18s incremental). Tasks tracked: 4 (all completed). Resolved dependency versions observed: tauri 2.11.5, tauri-macros/codegen/build 2.6.3, tauri-utils 2.9.3, c2pa 0.89, rustc 1.96.0, Vite 7.3.6.
roles_and_personas: Assistant operated as: (a) codebase archivist/documenter during /init; (b) frontend implementer (React/TS) for the wiring task; (c) Rust build-error diagnostician for the E0255 fault. User persona: solo maintainer (faizy) on Linux desktop, competent with Rust/Tauri/Bun/git, runs cargo via an 'rtk' caching wrapper, provides terminal output on request, prefers doing GUI/git actions themselves. No persona conflict or role drift.
prompting_strategies: User prompts were terse and artifact-driven: @-references to files (@docs/PROJECT.md, @docs/last-session.md, @docs/error.txt) as the primary context-passing mechanism; a slash command (/init); and a final schema-in-prompt request for structured output. Strategy relied on the assistant reading referenced files rather than re-explaining. Assistant strategy: read-before-act, verify-before-claim (ran real compiles instead of asserting success), and explicit hypothesis-testing (ruled out rtk/cache before landing on the pub-fn cause).
future_goals: 1) Migrate deprecated c2pa Reader::from_stream to Reader::from_context(ctx).with_stream(format, stream) against the verified 0.89 API (non-blocking warning). 2) Bundle a versioned trust list (PROJECT.md §10) so validly-signed files can reach ValidationState::Trusted -> Verified; without it, valid signatures fall to UntrustedOrBroken because c2pa ships no trust anchors. 3) Real end-to-end run via bun run tauri dev with test images (C2PA-signed vs ordinary photo). 4) Later phases: Phase 2 heuristic AI-artifact panel (ort/tract ONNX), Phase 3 video/audio, ingredient-chain visualization, trust-list staleness indicator.
style_guidelines: Two non-negotiable product rules enforced from PROJECT.md §2.3/§6/§11: (1) never blur C2PA cryptographic verification with heuristic AI detection — keep them visually/structurally separate; 'no manifest' is never evidence of anything. (2) Never use absolutist language ('this is AI/authentic'); use calibrated phrasing. Code style: match scaffold conventions (2-space indent, double quotes, snake_case Rust fields mirrored in TS). Comments reference the spec section they enforce. Assistant response style: concise, recommendation-first, honest about what was NOT done (e.g., did not launch the GUI).
session_scope: In scope: CLAUDE.md authoring, Phase 1 frontend wiring to analyze_media, and unblocking the Rust build. Out of scope this session: bundling the trust list, Phase 2 heuristic implementation, video/audio, git operations, and launching the GUI app (deferred to user's machine).
debug_events: Primary debug event: E0255 'the name __cmd__analyze_media / __tauri_command_name_analyze_media is defined multiple times' on cargo run during bun run tauri dev; user Ctrl+C'd citing slowness (actually first-time compile of c2pa's 100+ deps, not a hang). Investigation tree: verified single definition via grep + wc + cat -A (ruled out duplicated source); checked for stray use/re-export (none); tested plain cargo check to rule out the rtk cache wrapper (reproduced -> not rtk); checked tauri macro-crate version skew (consistent -> not skew); isolated the one structural difference vs the working stock template -> pub fn on the command. Fix applied (pub fn -> fn); cargo check exit 0. Prior-session debug lore carried in: rtk returns bogus fast/no-op results, so its output is not trusted for verification.
tone_fragments: User tone: laconic, pragmatic, trusting-but-verify ('It's Clean...', 'Hmm', 'had to end with ctrl+c, was taking too long'). No sarcasm, hostility, or drift detected. Occasional low-signal acknowledgements ('Hmm') treated as neutral continuation cues. Assistant tone: measured, diagnostic, non-defensive when its first theory (stale cache/rtk) was disproven — explicitly acknowledged the wrong hypothesis rather than glossing it.
model_adaptations: Model: claude-opus-4-8 (1M context), default effort. Adaptation: shifted from generative (writing UI code) to forensic (bisecting a macro-expansion error) mid-session. Notably corrected course after empirical disproof of the rtk/cache hypothesis, converging on the pub-fn cause via elimination. Emphasized running actual toolchain commands over asserting outcomes, consistent with the CLAUDE.md note that a passing typecheck/cargo check is not proof the wired app works.
tooling_context: Stack: Tauri v2, React 19, TypeScript ~5.8, Vite 7, Bun (frontend package manager), Rust edition 2021, rustc 1.96.0. Key crate: c2pa 0.89 pinned default-features=false, features=[file_io, rust_native_crypto] (no OpenSSL, no network by design). Tauri plugins: opener, dialog, fs (permissions in src-tauri/capabilities/default.json). Frontend APIs used: invoke from @tauri-apps/api/core, open from @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog, getCurrentWebview().onDragDropEvent from @tauri-apps/api/webview (native drag-drop yields real filesystem paths, unlike HTML5 DnD). Build/verify commands: bun run tauri dev (primary loop), bun run dev (vite only), bun run build, bunx tsc --noEmit, cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml. Caution: user's 'rtk' cargo wrapper caches aggressively and can report no-op results — bypass it to truly verify.
annotation_notes: IPC contract is the load-bearing structural fact: Rust AnalysisResult/Verdict (serde snake_case) must stay in sync with src/types.ts. Verdict serializes as 'verified' | 'untrusted_or_broken' | 'no_provenance'. Signer/claim_generator are extracted from reader.json() (JSON), NOT typed getters — deliberate resilience against c2pa's frequent minor-version breaks; keep new extraction JSON-based. IMAGE_EXTENSIONS in App.tsx must mirror mime_for_path() in lib.rs.
handoff_recommendations: Next agent: (1) run bun run tauri dev on the user's machine and drop a C2PA-signed image + an ordinary photo to confirm the three states render; (2) offer the from_stream -> from_context migration, verifying the exact 0.89 Context builder signature on docs.rs before editing; (3) when starting trust-list work, remember Verified is currently unreachable without bundled anchors. Do NOT re-add pub to the analyze_media command. Do NOT trust rtk's fast results — re-run plain cargo check. Preserve the C2PA-vs-heuristic separation and calibrated language in any new copy.
ethical_notes: The product's core ethic (and liability posture) is enforced in code, not just docs: absence of provenance is never presented as evidence of fakery/AI; heuristic guesses are structurally separated from cryptographic facts; no absolutist verdicts. The Phase 2 heuristic panel was shipped as a disabled, clearly-labeled 'non-authoritative' stub specifically to protect this separation structurally. Privacy: app makes no network calls by design; no telemetry; all analysis local. No PII handled beyond a local username in paths.
conversation_type: Multi-task software engineering session: documentation generation + feature implementation + build-failure debugging, followed by a meta request to produce this handoff artifact.
key_topics: C2PA provenance verification; three-state verdict model; Tauri IPC/command wiring; native drag-drop vs HTML5; React/TS frontend; serde<->TS type sync; tauri::command macro hygiene (E0255) and the pub-fn pitfall; c2pa 0.89 API and deprecation; offline/no-network design; trust-list absence; rtk caching-wrapper caveat.
session_boundaries: Start: /init request to author CLAUDE.md. Middle: 'continue from where left off' -> frontend wiring completed and verified. Then: @docs/error.txt -> diagnose+fix E0255. End: request to emit this structured handoff JSON. Clean, sequential boundaries; no overlap or abandoned threads. Model/skills/effort slash-command noise near the end carried an explicit caveat to ignore it.
